name: Wikipedia Race
organizer: Jaypee University of Information Technology (JUIT), Solan
about_content:
All that you need to know about
Wikipedia Race
Wikipedia Race is a fast-paced, intellectually stimulating event where participants navigate through Wikipedia pages to reach a target topic in the fewest possible clicks. It’s a fun blend of speed, logic, and knowledge that rewards players who can think creatively about how information is interconnected.
Beyond its entertainment value, the event encourages curiosity and quick research skills, teaching participants how to extract relevant information efficiently. The race transforms casual browsing into an engaging competition that celebrates the love for learning and discovery.

name: Call for Articles: Analytica November 2025
about_content:
All that you need to know about
Call for Articles: Analytica November 2025
Organon invites articles for the November 2025 edition of our magazine.
The event is open to all management verticals from B-Schools and professionals across India, including:
General Management
Marketing
Finance
Operations
Information Technology
Human Resources
Note:
The article should be original and related to Analytics & IT.
Key Details:
We are seeking original insights backed by references.
The best articles will be featured in our magazine and awarded a cash prize.
Results will be announced within 10-15 days post-28th November 2025.
Guidelines:
This is an individual event.
If there are issues regarding the submission link, please email your article to
analyticsclub@iimrohtak.ac.in
.
Mention your Name and Institute/Organization in the article.
Use the following file naming format: Individual's name_Organization name_Name of the article.
Format:
Font: Times New Roman
Size: 12
Word Limit: 1000-1500 words
File Type: .doc/.docx
Deadline for Submission: 28th November 2025
Rules:
Plagiarism will not be accepted.
Articles must be exclusively written for IIM Rohtak, with references mentioned.
All graphics, including images, graphs, and illustrations, should be of high quality.
Awards:
The winning entries will receive a total cash prize of Rs. 500 and e-certificates.
There is no participation certificate.

name: The Quantum Trial - Analytics Case Competition
organizer: Great Lakes Institute of Management (GLIM), Gurgaon
about_content:
All that you need to know about
The Quantum Trial - Analytics Case Competition
Round 1: Quiz Competition
Platform:
Live on Unstop
Dates:
5th – 8th November 2025
Content:
30 questions on Analytics concepts (moderate to tough)
Time:
15 minutes
Scoring:
+1 per correct answer (maximum 30 points)
Eligibility:
Open to all registered teams; one attempt per team
Advancement:
Top-scoring teams qualify for Round 2
Round 2: The Predictive Pulse – Turning Data into Retention (Online Submission)
Overview:
Teams will analyze a dataset based on the case study provided and present concise, data-driven insights and recommendations. Every insight must be supported by evidence and visualizations.
Submission Guidelines:
2-slide deck (excluding intro/thank-you slides)
Must include:
Key insights from the dataset on which the model will be built
Data-backed recommendations or managerial takeaways
Use any analytics or visualization tools
Keep slides clear, structured, and insight-focused
Upload the submission on Unstop before the deadline
Note:
Non-alignment between Round 2 and Round 3 will result in a deduction of marks.
Round 3: The Boardroom Challenge (On-Campus Presentation)
Finalists:
Top 15 teams from Round 2
Location:
Great Lakes Institute of Management, Gurgaon
Overview:
Finalists will present their complete analytical solution and recommendations before the judging panel.
Guidelines:
Submit an 8–10 slide presentation
Cover: problem understanding, methodology, insights, recommendations, and expected impact
Include a dashboard/AI model or visual summary (screenshots/visuals)
Use any analytics or visualization tools as per your solution and problem statement
Presentations will be made live before the judges
Awards:
Cash prizes and certificates for winners
prizes:
1st Prize: ₹25000 + Additional Course Coupons
2nd Prize: ₹15000 + Additional Course Coupons
3rd Prize: ₹10000 + Additional Course Coupons

name: Bottleneck Odyssey - OPUS x Grant Thornton
organizer: Great Lakes Institute of Management (GLIM), Gurgaon
about_content:
All that you need to know about
Bottleneck Odyssey - OPUS x Grant Thornton
Overview:
Embark on a strategic journey through the world of
operations, analytics, and business strategy
with

same academic batch

Winning Opportunity:
Get a chance to win
up to ₹2,60,000
as a team.
prizes:

name: Social Media Graphic & Content
about_content:
All that you need to know about
Social Media Graphic & Content
EthAum Venture Partners, a Singapore-based Deep Tech Revenue Accelerator and the fund is inviting all the students to participate in this '
Social Media Graphic & Content
' competition.
Please follow the below steps to participate:
You can use Canva, Photoshop, Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT or other AI tools to enhance the quality of overall graphic and text but stay factual
Check EthAum's website, f6S page LinkedIn page/posts, etc., to make your content/graphic relevant and impactful
Post the graphic and content on your LinkedIn page while tagging EthAum, and EthAum's three employees (Pankaj Gupta, Madhu Gupta and Elva Hendriks)
Garner impressions on your LinkedIn post by bringing likes/comments/reshares, etc.
Deadlines:
Register by Nov 19, 2025
Complete all the tasks by Nov 30, 2025
Submit screenshot of your post with impressions to startups@ethaum.com by Dec 5, 2025 with subject line, "Unstop challenge- Your Name"
Winning Criteria:
Complete all the steps mentioned above
Create high-quality graphic and content
The winner will be decided based on the quality of the post and number of impressions

name: Visyn
organizer: Kirori Mal College (KMC), University of Delhi, Delhi
about_content:
All that you need to know about
Visyn
Visyn
is a socio-economic innovation challenge that empowers students to think big, act bold, and create meaningful impact. This event calls upon young changemakers to design forward-looking solutions to real-world developmental issues that align with the vision of
Viksit Bharat 2047
— a progressive, inclusive, and sustainable India.
Through
Visyn
, students will have the chance to explore key socio-economic challenges, ideate creative interventions, and present their vision for a better tomorrow. It’s more than just a competition — it’s a platform to transform ideas into impact.
Who Can Participate:
Open to all students (undergraduate and postgraduate) across disciplines
What to Expect:
An exciting platform to showcase innovative solutions
Opportunities to ideate, collaborate, and gain visibility
Recognition and rewards for impactful ideas
Key Dates:
Preliminary Round: 14th November 2025
Intermediate Round: 19th November 2025
Grand Finale: 21st November 2025
Join us in reimagining the future.
Be part of Visyn — where students create the vision for impact.

name: The Healing Algorithms
about_content:
All that you need to know about
The Healing Algorithms
About:
The Healing Algorithms
is a competitive event that introduces participants to how artificial intelligence is transforming diagnosis, prediction, and decision-making in modern healthcare.
Participants will work with simplified medical datasets and apply core machine-learning techniques — such as regression, classification, and model evaluation — to solve clinically relevant tasks.
No advanced experience is required; the event is designed to be accessible yet challenging, encouraging logical thinking, data understanding, and creativity.
Through this event, students get a glimpse into
Medical Data Science
,
Bio-AI
, and how computational models can support doctors in identifying diseases, predicting patient outcomes, analyzing medical signals, and more.

name: Consultix
organizer: Kirori Mal College (KMC), University of Delhi, Delhi
about_content:
All that you need to know about
Consultix
Consultix
is an analytical and decision-making challenge that puts students in the shoes of consultants, tackling real-world business problems through data-driven insights and strategic thinking. Participants will analyze complex scenarios, craft innovative solutions, and present their recommendations with clarity and impact.
This challenge is the ultimate test of problem-solving, critical thinking, and teamwork — designed for those who aspire to make a mark in the world of consulting and strategy.
Consultix
is your opportunity to engage with India’s brightest young minds and experience how the next generation drives change through smart, strategic decisions.
Who Can Participate:
Open to all students (undergraduate and postgraduate) from any discipline
What to Expect:
Realistic business case challenges to crack
A platform to showcase analytical and strategic thinking
Opportunities for recognition, networking, and learning
Key Dates:
Preliminary Round: 14th November 2025
Intermediate Round: 19th November 2025
Grand Finale: 21st November 2025
Step up, think like a consultant, and redefine what’s possible.
Consultix – Crack the Case. Create the Change.

name: Meghadrive Eco-Rugged EV Challenge 2025
about_content:
All that you need to know about
Meghadrive Eco-Rugged EV Challenge 2025
Meghadrive Eco-Rugged EV Challenge 2025
Tagline:
“Engineering the Next Terrain-Ready Future”
Host:
Team Cognitia, E-Cell NIT Meghalaya and Institution Innovation Council (IIC)
Hashtag:
#Meghadrive2025
Registration Fees (Per Team of 1-3 and more members can also be added if requested):
Free
Event Overview:
The Meghadrive Eco-Rugged EV Challenge 2025 is a innovation competition focused on designing robust and sustainable electric vehicles tailored for the hilly and rural terrain of Northeast India. The challenge emphasizes scalable, cost-effective, and terrain-adapted mobility solutions to address high rainfall, steep gradients, and rural accessibility in Meghalaya and the Himalayan belt.
Competition Objective:
EV Design Challenge (EVDC)
– Design a virtual prototype of an electric vehicle suitable for Meghalaya's terrain.
Competition Structure
Round 1 – Abstract Submission (Online):
Submit a brief concept outlining your UAV design, objectives, and potential defence applications by maiing us on-
e-cell@nitm.ac.in
Round 2 – Final Presentation (Hybrid, 15th November):
Shortlisted teams will present their concepts, models, or prototypes before a panel of experts from
defence, academia, and industry
. We request to send the abstracts before the 14th for timely evaluation.
Vehicle Categories:
Electric 4-Wheeler for Passengers
Electric 4-Wheeler for Goods Transportation
Electric Mountain Bike (e-MTB)
Electric Rickshaw (e-Rickshaw)
Design Objectives:
Adaptability to Meghalaya and Himalayan terrain (gravel, rain, incline)
Use of sustainable materials (recycled or local)
Evaluation Parameters:
Terrain Adaptability & Suspension
Eco-Materials
Energy Efficiency (Battery usage/km)
Cost Optimization
Safety & Comfort
Cultural Aesthetics (Indian design elements in visuals)
Deliverables:
You can also present a concept or idea presentation — detailed prototypes and designs are not mandatory but are encouraged.
CAD Model (SolidWorks/Fusion360/Cero, etc.)
ANSYS/Simulink simulation (terrain & battery)
Technical Report (up to 15 pages)
Optional: 1:10 scale prototype
10-minute presentation
Timeline & Rewards:
Last Date of Abstract Submission:
14th November 2025 (11:59)
Winner:
₹30,000
Runner-Up:
₹15,000

name: Campus Ambassador Program
about_content:
All that you need to know about
Campus Ambassador Program
We are thrilled to launch the Samvaad 2026 – Campus Outreach Program
, the official outreach initiative of MDI Murshidabad’s flagship media and communication conclave, celebrating
“The Evolving World of Public Relations and Communication Strategy.”
The Program:
We’re looking for enthusiastic, creative, and responsible students with strong networking and communication skills to represent MDI Murshidabad on their campuses and promote Samvaad 2026 nationwide.
Responsibilities:
Promote Samvaad 2026 and drive registrations for the Case Competition on Unstop.
Create buzz through social media, WhatsApp, and student communities.
Engage clubs and societies to maximize participation.
Share weekly outreach updates.
Eligibility:
Open to all undergraduate and postgraduate students across India.
Students passionate about communication, marketing, PR, and event leadership are encouraged to apply.
Prior experience in campus events or ambassador programs is welcome but not required.
Perks & Benefits:
1st Place:
₹3,500 Cash Prize + Exclusive MDIM Merchandise + Certificate + Feature on MDI Murshidabad’s Official Social Media.
2nd Place:
₹1,500 Cash Prize + Exclusive MDIM Merchandise + Certificate + Social Media Feature.
3rd Place:
Exclusive MDIM Merchandise + Certificate + Social Media Feature.
All Participants:
Certificate of Participation from MDI Murshidabad.
Build your PR & marketing portfolio and gain real-world outreach experience.
Network with bright minds and professionals across India.
